items.headline: SB 79 presentation: state housing law overview for Sunnyvale
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission will hear a presentation on California's Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act (SB 79, 2025). The law affects how cities can zone and approve housing near transit, potentially reshaping Sunnyvale's development rules.

items.headline: 6-unit apartment building permitted on North 6th Street
items.summary: Alden Ventures received a permit for a new 6-unit apartment complex at 445 N 6th St, a multifamily project that adds to the city's housing stock in what appears to be a central location. At $879,706, this is the largest residential project in this batch.

items.headline: Tourism Improvement District renewed; Discover Santa Clara to manage
items.summary: Santa Clara renewed its Tourism Improvement District after a majority protest proceeding. Discover Santa Clara (the local DMO) will continue administering the district, which funds tourism promotion and development.

items.headline: Four parcels to be merged into one via tentative parcel map
items.summary: City Council acted on a vesting tentative parcel map (PLN25-00559) to consolidate four existing parcels. The source does not provide the location, size, intended use, or other details needed to assess impact on residents.

items.headline: Council to adopt park dedication ordinance for Measure E site
items.summary: The City Council is scheduled to adopt an ordinance dedicating a portion of the Measure E site as parkland on Tuesday, June 23. This converts part of a development project site into public park space.

items.headline: Baylands Golf Links driving range expansion feasibility reviewed
items.summary: The Council will hear a financial feasibility study update on expanding the driving range at Baylands Golf Links. The study session on Tuesday, June 23 will examine whether the expansion is financially viable.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission scheduled a study session on an update to Cupertino's Housing Element. The agenda does not specify what changes are being considered or what the update would add to the element.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: Sunnyvale's Planning Commission held a study session on June 22 to examine an artificial turf policy. This may lead to new rules on where or how artificial turf can be used in the city.
